assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Case A section shows upstream backport task with 'labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"]' and downstream propagation subtask with same labels. Linkage section shows: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, type: "Depend")' and 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Depend")'.

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md states: 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 has NO CVE Jira -- proactive preemptive remediation (Case B)'. remediation.md Case B section states: 'Cross-stream version impact analysis reveals that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0 in versions 2.1.0 and 2.1.1). A JQL search for sibling CVE Jiras with label CVE-2026-55123 in stream rhtpa-2.1 returns no results -- no CVE Jira exists for that stream.' Two preemptive tasks are created.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Preemptive Task 1 shows: 'labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]'. Preemptive Task 2 shows identical labels. cross-stream.md confirms: 'Labels: ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123, security-preemptive'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Linkage for Case B section states: 'Preemptive tasks use "Related" link type (not "Depend") to the originating CVE Jira, because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream (rhtpa-2.2):' followed by 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-upstream-task-key>, type: "Related")' and 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-downstream-task-key>, type: "Related")'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Preemptive Task 1 description contains: '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). > No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Preemptive Task 2 contains the identical prefix. cross-stream.md confirms: 'Each preemptive task description begins with: > **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2).'
